title: Fair Lending - Section 12
text: Section 12: Lenders shall not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, or age. All applicants must be evaluated solely on creditworthiness and objective criteria.

title: Income Verification - Section 34
text: Section 34: Income verification requirements for loan underwriting include pay stubs, bank statements, and tax returns when applicable.

title: Debt-to-Income Threshold Guidance
text: For conventional consumer lending, debt-to-income should generally remain at or below 0.45 unless a documented compensating factor is present and approved by senior underwriting.

title: Credit Score Minimums
text: Applicants below credit score 600 require adverse decision or manual review with documented exception criteria and compliance sign-off.

title: Adverse Action Notice Timing
text: Adverse action notices must be generated within regulatory response windows and include principal reasons for denial when credit is declined.

title: Automated Decision Oversight
text: Automated underwriting outputs with low evidence confidence must be flagged for human review before final customer communication.

title: Evidence Traceability Standard
text: Each model decision must store references to policy passages and confidence scores to enable independent audit reproducibility.

title: Manual Review Escalation
text: Escalate to reviewer queue when policy checks conflict or when citation quality falls below configured threshold values.

title: Income Stability Assessment
text: Income should be considered stable when verified history supports continuity over at least 24 months or equivalent contractual certainty.

title: Loan-to-Income Risk Signal
text: Applications with elevated loan-to-income ratio should trigger secondary scrutiny and require stronger supporting documentation.

title: Policy Version Governance
text: Every decision artifact must include the active policy version and configuration fingerprint used at decision time.

title: Reviewer Override Controls
text: Only authorized reviewer and admin roles may approve overrides on refused decisions, and all actions must be logged.

title: PII Logging Minimization
text: Personally identifiable fields in requests must be masked prior to persistence in diagnostic or audit systems.

title: State Overlay Policy - New York
text: New York product overlays require additional reviewer notes for high-risk profile declines and exception handling.

title: State Overlay Policy - California
text: California applications require verified disclosure readiness checks before final adverse action communication.

title: Counterfactual Explanation Guidance
text: When feasible, systems should provide actionable threshold guidance indicating what applicant factors would satisfy policy constraints.

title: Evidence Freshness Policy
text: Policy documents older than approved freshness windows should trigger stale-citation alerts in reviewer-facing interfaces.

title: Operational Resilience for LOS Integrations
text: LOS and CRM integration failures must not block reviewer access to queued decisions, and degraded mode should remain available.

title: Queue Export Compliance
text: Reviewer queue exports must preserve ticket identifiers, decision reasons, actions, and timestamps for audit traceability.

title: Risk Tier Prioritization
text: Open review tickets should be prioritized by potential consumer impact, policy severity, and unresolved compliance indicators.
